type.name: content_licensing_agreement
type.description: An agreement governing the licensing of audiovisual content (films, series, specials) from a rights holder to a licensee for distribution across specified media, territories, and time periods. The core transaction is a grant of exploitation rights in exchange for license fees, revenue shares, or minimum guarantees.

type.name: talent_performance_agreement
type.description: An agreement engaging an on-screen performer for a production, covering the performer's services, compensation (fixed and contingent), grant of rights in their performance and likeness, credit obligations, exclusivity, and termination provisions including pay-or-play terms.

type.name: music_synchronization_license
type.description: A license authorizing the use of a musical composition in timed synchronization with an audiovisual production. Covers the underlying composition only; a separate master use license is required for a specific recording. Defines approved use, duration, territory, media, and sync fee.

type.name: distribution_agreement
type.description: An agreement appointing a distributor to exploit content across specified channels (broadcast, cable, streaming, digital) within a territory. Covers distribution fees, recoupable expenses, windowing schedules, marketing approval, and reversion of rights upon termination.

type.name: digital_rights_ugc_license
type.description: An agreement between a digital platform and a content creator governing the upload, hosting, monetization, and distribution of user-generated content. Covers license grants, revenue sharing (ad, subscription, direct), content moderation, DMCA compliance, data portability, and platform amendment rights. Creator retains ownership.

category.name: grant_of_rights
category.description: The core operative provision transferring or licensing specific intellectual property rights from one party to another. Defines what the recipient can do with the content, where, on what platforms, and for how long.

category.name: exclusivity
category.description: Provisions restricting one or both parties from engaging with competitors or licensing to competing platforms. Includes holdbacks (restrictions on the licensor) and exclusivity periods (restrictions on the talent or creator).

category.name: compensation
category.description: All financial terms including fixed fees, variable compensation (revenue shares, royalties, residuals), minimum guarantees, and contingent compensation. The economic heart of the agreement.

category.name: term_and_termination
category.description: Provisions governing the duration of the agreement, renewal mechanics, grounds for early termination, cure periods, and what happens when the agreement ends (reversion, sell-off, final accounting).

category.name: representations_and_warranties
category.description: Statements of fact each party makes about their authority, the content, and their capabilities. Breach of a representation typically triggers indemnification obligations.

category.name: indemnification
category.description: Obligations to defend and hold harmless the other party from claims arising from breach of representations, warranties, or obligations. May be mutual or asymmetric.

term.name: SVOD
term.definition: Subscription Video-on-Demand. A platform where users pay a recurring subscription fee for access to a content library (e.g., Netflix, Disney+).

term.name: AVOD
term.definition: Advertising-based Video-on-Demand. A platform offering free content supported by advertising (e.g., Tubi, Pluto TV).

term.name: TVOD
term.definition: Transactional Video-on-Demand. A platform where users pay per-title to rent or purchase content (e.g., iTunes, Google Play).

term.name: FAST
term.definition: Free Ad-Supported Streaming TV. Linear-style streaming channels supported by advertising, mimicking traditional broadcast (e.g., Pluto TV channels, Tubi channels).

term.name: holdback
term.definition: A contractual restriction preventing the licensor from licensing the same content to specified competing platforms or services within the territory during a defined period.

term.name: windowing
term.definition: The practice of releasing content sequentially across different distribution platforms, with each window having an exclusivity period before the next window opens. Typical sequence: theatrical, premium VOD, SVOD, free TV.

term.name: minimum_guarantee
term.definition: A guaranteed minimum payment from licensee to licensor, typically recoupable against future revenue share payments. Functions as a floor on compensation or an advance against earnings.

term.name: most_favored_nations
term.definition: A provision ensuring one party receives terms no less favorable than those granted to any comparable party in a similar transaction. Common in music sync deals to ensure parity among compositions used in the same production.

term.name: pay_or_play
term.definition: A commitment by the producer to pay the performer's full fixed compensation regardless of whether the performer's services are actually utilized. Protects the performer if the production is cancelled or the role is recast.

term.name: residuals
term.definition: Ongoing payments to performers, directors, or writers for reuse or continued exploitation of a production beyond its initial exhibition. Often governed by guild collective bargaining agreements (SAG-AFTRA, DGA, WGA).

term.name: synchronization_license
term.definition: A license to use a musical composition in timed synchronization with visual images in an audiovisual production. Covers the underlying composition; a separate master use license is needed for a specific sound recording.

term.name: master_use_license
term.definition: A license to use a specific sound recording of a musical composition, obtained from the record label or master rights holder. Required in addition to the synchronization license when using an existing recording.

term.name: net_revenue
term.definition: Gross revenue less specified deductions (taxes, platform fees, approved expenses). The exact definition varies by contract and is a critical negotiation point — the approved deductions determine the actual economic value.

term.name: gross_receipts
term.definition: Total revenue received by the distributor from exploitation of the content, before any deductions. The starting point from which distribution fees and recoupable expenses are subtracted.

term.name: net_profits
term.definition: Revenue remaining after deduction of all production costs, distribution fees, overhead charges, and other contractually defined expenses. Historically one of the most contentious defined terms in entertainment agreements.

term.name: reversion
term.definition: The return of all licensed rights to the original rights holder upon expiration or termination of the agreement. The licensee/distributor must cease exploitation and the rights holder regains full control.

term.name: sell_off_period
term.definition: A limited period following termination during which the distributor may fulfill existing commitments made prior to the termination notice. Prevents abrupt disruption of existing distribution arrangements.

term.name: force_majeure
term.definition: A contractual provision excusing performance when prevented by extraordinary events beyond reasonable control, such as natural disasters, war, pandemic, government action, or infrastructure failure.

term.name: morals_clause
term.definition: A provision allowing termination or other remedies if a party (typically talent) engages in conduct that brings them into public disrepute or scandal, or that materially and adversely affects the commercial value of the production.

term.name: DMCA
term.definition: Digital Millennium Copyright Act. U.S. federal law establishing a framework for copyright protection in digital environments, including safe harbor provisions for platforms that comply with notice-and-takedown procedures.

term.name: pro_rata_share
term.definition: In subscription revenue models, a creator's share calculated proportionally based on their content's viewing time relative to total platform viewing time, applied against a defined percentage of the subscription revenue pool.
